series_metadata.series_description: reports filed with the State Board of Elections by candidates, referendum committees, and legal expense funds; includes analyses of campaign committees’ finances

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: certifications prepared by the State Board of Elections and filed with the Department of the Secretary of State; includes notices of candidacy, certificates of nomination or election, and certificates of results of referenda and elections

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: abstracts of votes compiled by the county boards of elections after completion of county canvases and maintained by the Department of the Secretary of State

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: nominations filed with the Department of the Secretary of State

records concerning requests to the State Board of Elections to have name printed on ballot as an unaffiliated candidate, to qualify as a write-in candidate, to recognize a new political party, or to be endorsed by a national political party for the office of President of the United States

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_title: Precinct Boundary Maps NOTE: In North Carolina, the General Assembly is responsible for drawing congressional as well as state legislative and judicial district lines.
series_metadata.series_description: alterations to approved precinct boundaries as requested by county boards of elections and reviewed by the Executive Director of the State Board of Elections

series_metadata.series_title: Precinct Boundary Maps NOTE: In North Carolina, the General Assembly is responsible for drawing congressional as well as state legislative and judicial district lines.
series_metadata.series_description: proposed alterations to precinct boundaries that are rejected by the Executive Director of the State Board of Elections

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Rejection PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: original abstracts and certifications of votes of the state prepared by the State Board of Elections and maintained by the Department of the Secretary of State

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: submissions of NC legislative changes that are required to be filed for the General Assembly with the U.S. Department of Justice by the Administrative Office of the Courts, NC Department of Justice, State Board of Elections, State Board of Education, or the Secretary of State

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning awards from the Office of the Governor

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center at the end of the administration for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: requests to the Office of the Governor for reductions in criminal sentences; includes applications, correspondence, briefs, recommendations, and reports
series_metadata.legal_citation: Confidentiality G.S. § 148-74 G.S. § 148-76 Goble v. Bounds (13 N.C. App. 579)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends. Records will be held for agency 75 additional years and then transferred to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning the memoranda, correspondence, and other related records received from and sent to the Governor and Lieutenant Governor, other officials within the offices, and state and federal agencies concerning the administration and management of the office

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center at the end of the administration for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records related to the activities of the Offices of the Governor and Lieutenant Governor with regards to communications with constituents

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center at the end of the administration for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning appointments by the Offices of the Governor and Lieutenant Governor to agencies, boards, commissions, and judiciary; includes letters of appointment, correspondence, biographical summaries, oaths of office, letters of recommendation and resignation, and other related records SEE ALSO: , (below) Appointments to ServiceGoverning and Advisory Body Member Files

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center at the end of the administration for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_title: Executive Orders, Disaster Declarations, and Proclamations
series_metadata.series_description: records from the Office of the Governor related to the issuance of executive orders, disaster declarations, and proclamations; also includes orders placing the National Guard in active service SEE ALSO: Disaster Recovery Records (RISK MANAGEMENT)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center at the end of the Governor’s administration for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records from the Office of the Governor concerning the legal surrender of an alleged criminal subject to extradition; includes correspondence, briefs, summaries, waivers, executive agreements, and other related records; also includes documentation of rewards offered
series_metadata.legal_citation: Confidentiality G.S. § 148-74 G.S. § 148-76 Goble v. Bounds (13 N.C. App. 579)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer annually to the State Records Center for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records from the Offices of the Governor and Lieutenant Governor that document the intergovernmental and external relations and activities with state and local officials, organizations, and other state and federal entities

tracking_data.comments: Destroy records relating to non-policy and short-term issues when reference value ends. Transfer remaining records to the State Records Center at the end of the administration for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records related to proposed and enacted legislative activities of the Offices of the Governor and Lieutenant Governor

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center at the end of the administration for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records related to the work of the ombudsman in the Office of the Governor SEE ALSO: Improper Conduct Investigations (MONITORING AND COMPLIANCE)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center at the end of the Governor’s administration for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records documenting the policies developed and enacted by the Offices of the Governor and Lieutenant Governor; also includes legal work of the offices
series_metadata.legal_citation: Confidentiality G.S. § 148-74 G.S. § 148-76 Goble v. Bounds (13 N.C. App. 579)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center at the end of the Governor’s administration for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: schedules of the Governor, First Spouse, and Lieutenant Governor

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center at the end of the administration for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records related to the activities of the Offices of the Governor and Lieutenant Governor with regards to communications through speeches, interviews, press releases, and briefings

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center at the end of the administration for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records related to the activities of the Offices of the Governor and Lieutenant Governor that document strategic planning and initiatives promoted by the administration

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center at the end of the administration for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records maintained by the Department of the Secretary of State concerning appointments or reappointments issued by the Office of the Governor, Lieutenant Governor, members of the General Assembly, or other appointing authority; includes letters and notices/ commissions of appointments and oaths of office of board and commission members; also includes resignations from the General Assembly SEE ALSO: (below), (above) Oaths of OfficeExecutive Appointments

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning appointments or reappointments issued by the agency SEE ALSO: (below), (above) Oaths of OfficeExecutive Appointments

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Service ends PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records related to selections of members; includes recommendations or nominations and other related records SEE ALSO: (below), (above) Oaths of OfficeExecutive Appointments

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Reference value ends ± THEN: Destroy Agency Policy: ________________

series_metadata.series_description: documents defining the formal organization and standing rules of governing bodies
internal and external communications of board members related to decision-making, policy development, and other high-level planning SEE ALSO: Correspondence (AGENCY MANAGEMENT)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: transitory correspondence of board members SEE ALSO: Correspondence (AGENCY MANAGEMENT)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Received/Sent PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: historical biographical information on members; includes resumes/CVs and photographs SEE ALSO: Correspondence (AGENCY MANAGEMENT)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: routine biographical information on members; includes resumes/CVs and photographs SEE ALSO: Correspondence (AGENCY MANAGEMENT)

series_metadata.series_title: Governing and Advisory Body Minutes (continued on following page)
series_metadata.series_description: approved minutes for governing or advisory bodies; includes minutes, attachments, and addenda

official copies of oaths taken by various state personnel as required by law and maintained by the Department of the Secretary of State; also includes oaths of office by members of the General Assembly SEE ALSO: (above) Appointments to Service

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning personal and financial interest disclosures filed with the State Ethics Commission by individuals covered under the State Government Ethics Act

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 10 years ΩTHEN: Destroy

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center 5 years after December 31 of the filing year. Records will be held for agency 5 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: State of North Carolina’s rules submitted by state agencies to the Office of Administrative Hearings; includes official signed submission forms and original copies of rule text as adopted; also includes Rules Review Commission notebooks
messages exchanged by the NC House of Representatives and the NC Senate

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records with historical significance produced in the course of liaising with other government officials regarding agency priorities

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: routine government affairs records; includes legislative liaisons

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Reference value ends ± THEN: Destroy Agency Policy: ________________

series_metadata.series_description: daily records of official proceedings of the NC House of Representatives and the NC Senate; also includes lists of members attending, business transacted at sessions, calendars, and other supporting documents

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: includes official copies of session law, ratified acts, and resolutions enacted by the General Assembly and maintained by the Department of the Secretary of State; also includes failed to pass bills that were not enrolled by the General Assembly

tracking_data.comments: Transfer electronic recordings to the State Records Center at the end of each biennium session for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives. Transfer paper records to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records documenting legislative committees, study committees, standing, and non-standing committees in the General Assembly; includes reports, minutes, presentations, testimony, votes, correspondence, memoranda, and other related records SEE ALSO: Reporting (AGENCY MANAGEMENT)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends. Records will be held for agency 25 additional years and then transferred to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: original petitions delivered to the General Assembly SEE ALSO: Constituent Comments, Complaints, and Petitions (PUBLIC RELATIONS)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records identifying members present and absent from General Assembly sessions and results of electronic voting

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center after 2 years to be microfilmed. Paper records will be destroyed after microfilming. Microfilm will be transferred to custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_title: Rule Making Proceedings Note: Fiscal notes and certifications produced by the Office of State Budget and Management are required to be retained by the requesting agency.
series_metadata.series_description: records concerning rule making proceedings within the agency; includes written comments and petitions received, transcripts or recordings of public hearings, certifications, fiscal notes, and written explanations for adopting the rule SEE ALSO: Official Publications (PUBLIC RELATIONS)
